exercises.name: Pilates TVA SL elevated hip knee tuck
exercises.description: If you do not have a yoga block you can use a volleyball or a towel rolled up

exercises.name: Pilates unstable knee to elbow
exercises.description: If you do not have a balancing pillow you can use a rolled up towel or a yoga ball.

exercises.name: Pilates v sit w/ ab circles both directions
Pilates V sit w/ SB twists
exercises.description: Keep abs pulled IN, hold your pee contraction, and use inner thighs to keep tension on ball.

exercises.name: Pilates V towel holds lower leg raises
exercises.description: You can use a yoga block, towel, or ball between knees. Squeeze knees together and Kegel. I want you to pull core in deep, making breathe shallow

exercises.name: Pilates wt alt hand to knee
Pistol Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright with your arms extended in front.

2.Lower your body towards the floor sending your hips back and down, keeping the raised leg and arms straight out in front.

3.Push off the standing leg to return to upright position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Pistol Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright with one foot slightly off the floor.

2.Slowly lower your body into a squat position on the standing foot, keeping your back neutral/flat and the other foot off the floor.

3Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Pistol Squat to Sit
exercises.description:
1.Stand with one knee and toe slightly pointing away from the body. Arms extended in front.

2.Lower your body towards the medicine ball, sending your hips back and down, raising one leg, arms extended in front.

3.Push off the other leg to return to starting position.

4. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Plank Alternating Arm & Leg Lift
exercises.description:
1.Support your body off the floor, resting on your toes and elbows.

2.Raise one arm and the opposite leg straight up about 18 inches off the floor.

3.Hold briefly, then lower your leg and arm back to the floor returning to starting position. 

4.Repeat with the other arm and opposite leg. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Plank Alternating Arm Reach
exercises.description: From a low plank position, reach one arm forward, maintaining stability in the body and engaging the core.

exercises.name: Plank Alternating Leg Lift
exercises.description:
1.Raise your body off the floor on your elbows, resting on your toes.

2.Raise one leg straight up off the floor, keeping your body in a straight line and your back neutral/flat.

3.Hold briefly, then lower back your leg to the floor returning to starting position.

4.Raise the opposite leg. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Plank Hip Twist
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, keep your abdominals active and glutes on. 

2) Start getting into a plank position. Elbows beneath your shoulders and a nice straight line throughout your body. 

3) Slowly twist, dropping your hip from side to side. 

4) The goal is to twist your body as much as you can to get a full rotation.

exercises.name: Plank jacks w/ plank hold
exercises.description: 10 jacks right into 10 sec plank hold back into 10 jack, plank, jacks, plank. Etc. number of reps is is the amount of times you go through that pattern. For example 3 reps means plank jacks, plank, plank jacks plank, plank jacks plank.

exercises.name: Plank Pull-Through
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position with a dumbbell placed on the floor to one side of your body. 

Keep your hands under your shoulders, core tight, and body in a straight line from head to heels. 

Reach across with the opposite hand to pull the dumbbell under your body to the other side. 

Return your hand to the starting position while keeping your hips as stable as possible. Alternate sides for the desired number of reps.

exercises.name: Plank Pull-Through to Push up
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position with a dumbbell placed on the floor to one side of your body. 

Keep your hands under your shoulders, core tight, and body in a straight line from head to heels. Reach across with the opposite hand to pull the dumbbell under your body to the other side. 

After completing the pull-through, lower your chest to the floor to perform a push-up, then press back up to the plank position. 

Alternate the dumbbell pull-through with push-ups for the desired number of reps.

exercises.name: Plank To Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a mat with your legs straight and arms tucked in by your sides with elbows bent.

2.Raise your body up into the top of a push up position, resting on your hand and toes and hold briefly. Keep your back flat and head looking down at the floor.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat required reps.

exercises.name: Plank with Alternating Reach Through
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a mat with elbows resting on the floor next to your chest, palms facing forward or in a fist position, feet together.

2.Push your body off the floor in a pushup position with your body resting on elbows.

3.Keeping the body in a straight line from head to toes, lean on to one arm while the other arm passes through.

4.Return to starting position.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side. Repeat.

exercises.name: Plate Floor Glute Bridge
exercises.alternateName: Floor Hip Thruster with a plate
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you'll want the work focused on your glutes rather than your lower back.

2. Ensure that you have your abdominals active by the time you reached the top of the glute bridge.

3. Begin lying on the ground, knees bent, with a plate over your legs. This will be your starting position.

4. Begin the movement by driving through with your heels, extending your hips vertically keeping the plate in place. Your weight should be supported by your upper back and the heels of your feet.

5. Extend as far as possible.

exercises.name: Plate Hip Thrust
exercises.alternateName: Bench Hip Thruster with a Plate
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, maintain a neutral spine  and lift the plate from the floor with your glutes rather than your lower back.

2. Keep your abdominals engaged and pulled in.

3. Begin by sitting close to a bench, arms spread wide. This will be your starting position.

4. Begin the movement by driving through with your heels, extending your hips vertically keeping the plate in place. Your weight should be supported by your upper back and the heels of your feet.

5. Extend as far as possible.

exercises.name: Plate Push
exercises.description: Plate Push

exercises.name: Plate Russian Twist
exercises.description:
1) Sit on the floor, and with both hands hold a plate out in front you with arms slightly bent. 

2) Slightly lean your upper body backwards, until it's against the floor. 

3) Begin by curling your body up, and twisting to the left while keeping the plate held out in front. 

4) Pause, and slowly return to start. Repeat with other side.

exercises.name: Plate slide reverse lunges
exercises.description: The glute muscle from the leg in front should be driving this motion. If you can not feel it lean forward slightly.

exercises.name: Plate Weighted Crunch
exercises.description:
1.Lie flat on your back holding a weight extended above your body, arms straight., your feet flat on the ground, knees bent at a 90-degree angle.

2.Slowly get off the floor bringing your shoulders towards your knees.

3.Lower down your shoulders returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Plate Weighted Dip
exercises.description:
1) Strap a weighted dip belt around your waist and grip the dip bars shoulder width apart with arms straight. 

2) Lower your body until you feel a stretch in your shoulders. Then push body back up to the starting position. 

3) Repeat.
